title: Characterization of Constraints in Flexible Unknown Environments
summary: This paper presents an online path planning algorithm for safe autonomous manipulation of a flexibly constrained object in an unknown environment. Methods for real time identification and characterization of perceived flexible constraints and global stiffness are presented. Used in tandem, these methods allow a robot to simultaneously explore, characterize, and manipulate an elastic system safely. Navigation without a-priori knowledge of the system is achieved using constraint exploration based on local force and position information. The perceived constraint stiffness is considered at multiple poses along an object's (system) trajectory. Using stiffness eigenvector information, global stiffness behavior is characterized and identified using an atlas of simple mechanical constraints, such as hinges and planar constraints. Validation of these algorithms is carried out by simulation and experimentally. The ability to recognize several common simple mechanical constraints (such as a flexible hinge) in real time, and to subsequently identify relevant screw parameters is demonstrated. These results suggest the feasibility of simultaneous global constrain/stiffness exploration and safe manipulation of flexibly constrained objects. We believe that this approach will eventually enable safe cooperative manipulation in applications such as organ retraction and manipulation during surgery
tldr: This paper introduces an online path planning algorithm for autonomous manipulation of flexibly constrained objects in unknown environments, featuring real-time identification of flexible constraints and global stiffness. It demonstrates feasibility in recognizing common mechanical constraints and safe manipulation, with potential applications in surgical robotics.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种在线路径规划算法，用于在未知环境中自主操作柔性约束物体，实时识别柔性约束和全局刚度。展示了识别常见机械约束和安全操作的可行性，潜在应用于手术机器人。
summary_zh: 本文提出了一种用于未知环境中柔性约束物体安全自主操作的在线路径规划算法。研究提出了实时识别与表征感知柔性约束及全局刚度的方法。这些方法协同使用时，可使机器人安全地同步完成弹性系统的探索、表征与操作任务。通过基于局部力和位置信息的约束探索，实现了无需系统先验知识的导航。研究在物体（系统）运动轨迹的多个位姿处考量感知约束刚度，利用刚度特征向量信息，通过简单机械约束（如铰链和平面约束）的图册来表征和识别全局刚度行为。通过仿真和实验验证了这些算法的有效性，实时识别多种常见简单机械约束（如柔性铰链）并确定相关螺旋参数的能力得到证实。研究结果表明，同步实现全局约束/刚度探索与柔性约束物体安全操作具有可行性。我们相信该方法最终将能实现手术中器官牵拉等应用场景下的安全协同操作。

title: Training-free Detection and 6D Pose Estimation of Unseen Surgical Instruments
summary: Purpose: Accurate detection and 6D pose estimation of surgical instruments are crucial for many computer-assisted interventions. However, supervised methods lack flexibility for new or unseen tools and require extensive annotated data. This work introduces a training-free pipeline for accurate multi-view 6D pose estimation of unseen surgical instruments, which only requires a textured CAD model as prior knowledge. Methods: Our pipeline consists of two main stages. First, for detection, we generate object mask proposals in each view and score their similarity to rendered templates using a pre-trained feature extractor. Detections are matched across views, triangulated into 3D instance candidates, and filtered using multi-view geometric consistency. Second, for pose estimation, a set of pose hypotheses is iteratively refined and scored using feature-metric scores with cross-view attention. The best hypothesis undergoes a final refinement using a novel multi-view, occlusion-aware contour registration, which minimizes reprojection errors of unoccluded contour points. Results: The proposed method was rigorously evaluated on real-world surgical data from the MVPSP dataset. The method achieves millimeter-accurate pose estimates that are on par with supervised methods under controlled conditions, while maintaining full generalization to unseen instruments. These results demonstrate the feasibility of training-free, marker-less detection and tracking in surgical scenes, and highlight the unique challenges in surgical environments. Conclusion: We present a novel and flexible pipeline that effectively combines state-of-the-art foundational models, multi-view geometry, and contour-based refinement for high-accuracy 6D pose estimation of surgical instruments without task-specific training. This approach enables robust instrument tracking and scene understanding in dynamic clinical environments.
tldr: The paper introduces a training-free pipeline for 6D pose estimation of unseen surgical instruments using multi-view geometry and contour-based refinement, achieving accuracy comparable to supervised methods without requiring annotated data.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了一种无需训练的流程，用于通过多视角几何和基于轮廓的细化来估计未见手术器械的6D姿态，无需标注数据即可达到与监督方法相当的精度。
summary_zh:
摘要：
目的：手术器械的精确检测与六维姿态估计对众多计算机辅助介入技术至关重要。然而，有监督方法对新器械或未见器械缺乏适应性，且需要大量标注数据。本研究提出一种无需训练的流程，仅需纹理化CAD模型作为先验知识，即可实现未见手术器械的多视角六维姿态精确估计。方法：该流程包含两个核心阶段。首先在检测阶段，通过预训练特征提取器生成各视角下的物体掩膜提案并计算其与渲染模板的相似度评分，通过跨视角匹配检测结果、三角化为三维实例候选，并利用多视角几何一致性进行筛选。其次在姿态估计阶段，采用带跨视角注意力的特征度量评分对姿态假设集进行迭代优化与评分，最终通过新型多视角遮挡感知轮廓配准（最小化未遮挡轮廓点的重投影误差）完成最优假设的精细化调整。结果：在MVPSP数据集真实手术场景下的严格测试表明，该方法在受控条件下可获得与有监督方法相当的毫米级精度姿态估计，同时保持对未见器械的完全泛化能力。这些结果验证了手术场景中无需训练、无标记物检测与跟踪的可行性，并揭示了手术环境特有的技术挑战。结论：我们提出了一种创新且灵活的流程，通过融合前沿基础模型、多视角几何与基于轮廓的精细化策略，无需任务特定训练即可实现手术器械的高精度六维姿态估计。该方法为动态临床环境中的器械追踪与场景理解提供了鲁棒解决方案。
